title: Spiralverse Master Pack Integration (RWP v2.1)
content:
# Spiralverse Master Pack Integration (RWP v2.1)

Spiralverse Master Pack Integration
Real World Protocol v2.1 Unified Envelope
"Invitation over Command, Connection over Control."
Executive Summary
The Spiralverse Master Pack (v2.2) is the production-ready envelope layer that unifies SCBE, GeoSeal, and Symphonic Cipher into a single, themed, interoperable protocol carrying intent, time/place, and security guarantees between AI providers while preserving creative identity.
Value Proposition:
Security: AES-256-GCM / ChaCha20-Poly1305 with HKDF-SHA256, AAD-bound metadata
Resilience: Six-stage verification pipeline, graceful degradation, provider fallbacks
Observability: Phase skew, trust scores, rejection/decay, envelope telemetry with alerts
Scalability: Harmonic H(d,R) complexity maps depth → pricing
IP Moat: Six Languages unify technical protocol + worldbuilding
Security & Envelope Architecture (RWP v2.1)
Core Primitives (Standard, Not Invented)
Encryption:
Primary: AES-256-GCM (hardware accelerated)
Fallback: ChaCha20-Poly1305 (low-power / no-AES hardware)
Key Derivation:
HKDF-SHA256(
  IKM = master,
  salt = nonce || context,
  info = "spiralverse/rwp2/v2"
)
AAD (Authenticated Associated Data):
Everything not secret is bound: origin tongue, timestamp, sequence, phase, intent, provider, version
Canonicalization:
JSON Canonical Form (sorted keys, UTF-8 NFC, no trailing zeros)
Hash canonical form for signatures
Deterministic envelope: BLAKE3/SHA-256 commit hash anchored in headers
Minimal RWP v2.1 Frame
{
  "ver": "2.1",
  "tongue": "KO|AV|RU|CA|UM|DR",
  "origin": "provider-id",
  "ts": "2026-01-05T12:00:00Z",
  "seq": 42,
  "phase": "schema|fractal|intent|trajectory|phase|neural|swarm|crypto",
  "aad": "context=task:uuid;trace=uuid;golden_phi=1.618",
  "payload": "<Base64URL>",
  "enc": "aes-256-gcm",
  "kid": "rwp2:keyring:v1",
  "nonce": "<96-bit>",
  "sig": "<HMAC-SHA256 over canonical(AAD+payload)>"
}
Changes from legacy: XOR removed; all metadata moved to AAD; keys derived via HKDF; normalized harmonic features to prevent length leaks
Dual-Door Consensus & Roundtable Tiers
Mental Model
"Two keys, two doors, two rooms wave each other in" — prevents unilateral execution and creates provable separation of duties
Roundtable Tiers (Per Operation Class)
Dual-Door Handshake
Door A (Ops) issues challenge A  ─┐
Door B (Security) issues challenge B ─┤→ agent must satisfy both via AAD-bound proofs
Room A discloses "who's in the box"  ─┘
(one at a time) → Roundtable verifies quorum → allow
Implementation: Use standard multisig (Ed25519/Ed448, or threshold BLS) over canonical envelope hash. Never invent custom signature schemes.
Triple-Helix Key Schedule
Safe rotation design using proven ciphers with predictable context rotation keyed by coprime periods.
Three Helices
Helix A (Time): 
slot_index = floor(timestamp / Δ)
Δ typically 5–15 minutes
Helix B (Intention):
Map intent → domain (coord|io|policy|compute|secrets|schema) → index i
Helix C (Place/Provider):
Provider shard index p (e.g., chatgpt=1, grok=2, …)
Context Selector
ring = (a*t + b*i + c*p + seed) mod M
cipher = (ring % 2 == 0) ? AES-GCM : ChaCha20-Poly1305
info = "rwp2/v2:" + tongue + ":" + phase + ":" + ring
key = HKDF(master, salt=nonce||ring, info=info)
Pick M as product of coprimes (e.g., 47×61×73 = 209,111). This yields long apparent cycles without relying on made-up crypto.
Six-Language DSL → Workflow Mapping
TypeScript Mapping Example
export const tongueForNode: Record<NodeType, Tongue> = {
  condition: 'aelindra',
  loop: 'aelindra',
  branch: 'aelindra',
  http: 'voxmara',
  webhook: 'voxmara',
  email: 'voxmara',
  variable: 'thalassic',
  context: 'thalassic',
  math: 'numerith',
  filter: 'numerith',
  encrypt: 'glyphara',
  hash: 'glyphara',
  format: 'glyphara',
  validate: 'morphael',
  transform: 'morphael',
};
6D Vector Navigation & Proximity Optimization (v2.2)
Axes
Primary: X/Y/Z (AXIOM/FLOW/GLYPH)
Secondary: V/H/S (ORACLE/CHARM/LEDGER)
Protocol Complexity Scaling
Tight formations: Use 1–2 tongues → 70–80% bandwidth savings
Far agents: Use full 6-tongue protocol
Auto-locking dock: When V (velocity) & S (security) converge → ephemeral session keys
Distance-Adaptive Protocol
def select_protocol_complexity(distance_6d: float) -> int:
    if distance_6d < 0.1:  # Tight formation
        return 1  # Minimal protocol
    elif distance_6d < 0.5:
        return 3  # Medium protocol
    else:
        return 6  # Full protocol
Sentinel & Steward Loop — Ops Runbook
Daily (≤15 min)
Front-Door Gate: Review last 24h of allow/review/deny and spot spikes
Roundtable Queue: Clear review items (dual-door approvals)
Trust & Decay: Check new auto-exclusions; re-admit only with Steward sign-off
Phase Health: Scan phase-skew and rejection outliers; file ticket if >3σ
Anomaly Feed: Label 5 events for tomorrow's retrain
Weekly (30–45 min)
Scorecard: Review SLOs; tune thresholds; add one new guardrail
Drill: Run "deny but benign" exercise; confirm graceful degradation
SLOs / Guardrails
Severity → Action
Sev-3: Anomaly spike or skew>p99 → raise review threshold + notify on-call
Sev-2: Verify failures>0.1% 5-min window → fail-to-noise + degrade non-critical
Sev-1: Crypto error or key drift → pause outgoing, rotate keys, Roundtable Tier-4
Integration with AETHERMOORE Layers
[User Interface]
    ↓
[SCBE Context Collector] ← (timestamp, device, threat, entropy, load, stability)
    ↓
[API Gateway with RWP v2.1 envelopes]
    ↓
[Orchestrator + SCBE Intent Verifier]
    ├─ Verify context commitment
    ├─ Check neural behavior pattern
    ├─ Validate trajectory alignment
    ├─ Lock to phase cycle
    └─ Check swarm consensus
    ↓
[Encrypted routing based on Intent]
    ├─ OpenAI (sil'kor: Foundation)
    ├─ Google AI (nav'een: Journey)
    ├─ Anthropic (thel'vori: Transformation)
    └─ Fleet Engine [Swarm Consensus Layer]
Configuration Examples
Sentinel Agents (YAML)
sentinels:
  - name: phase-skew
    source: telemetry.phase_skew_ms
    window: "5m"
    threshold:
      p95: 150
    action:
      on_breach: raise_severity: SEV-3
      
  - name: verify-fail
    source: telemetry.verify_failure_rate
    window: "1m"
    threshold:
      rate_gt: 0.001  # 0.1%
    action:
      on_breach: trigger_fail_to_noise: true
Front-Door Gate Policy
gate:
  min_wait_ms: 100
  max_wait_ms: 5000
  alpha: 1.5
  review_threshold: 0.5
  allow_threshold: 0.8
  roundtable:
    tier_map:
      low:   ["KO"]
      medium: ["KO","RU"]
      high:  ["KO","RU","UM"]
      crit:  ["KO","RU","UM","DR"]
Envelope Example
{
  "spelltext": "AXIOM<origin>KO</origin><seq>42</seq><ts>2026-01-05T12:00:00Z</ts>",
  "payload": "eyJhY3Rpb24iOiJtb3ZlX2FybSIsInBhcmFtcyI6eyJ4IjoxMCwieSI6MjAsInoiOjV9fQ==",
  "sigs": {
    "KO": "a7f2c9...",
    "RU": "d4e1b8...",
    "UM": "3f9a2c..."
  }
}
Glossary (Quick Reference)
AAD: Authenticated Associated Data (signed, not encrypted)
Fail-to-noise: On security anomaly, return harmless noise instead of error details
Roundtable: Multi-signature policy gates by domain tongue
Helix schedule: Deterministic context rotation across time/intent/place
Six Languages: Aelindra, Voxmara, Thalassic, Numerith, Glyphara, Morphael
SCBE: Schema→…→Crypto verification conveyor for envelopes
RWP: Real World Protocol (envelope specification)
Status
✅ Security envelope architecture: Complete
✅ Dual-door consensus: Specified
✅ Triple-helix key schedule: Designed
✅ Six-language DSL mapping: Complete
✅ Sentinel/Steward runbook: Documented
🔄 Production deployment: In progress
🔄 Integration testing: Ongoing
Integration Points:
